steps.title: Identify the needed generalization
steps.content: The student wants to make and support a generalization about exoplanets. The notes include masses and host stars, but the useful pattern is about names: Phobetor is drawn from Greek mythology, while Mastika means "gem" or "jewel" in Malay. Those two examples support a broad claim about exoplanet names having diverse origins.

steps.title: Eliminate unsupported choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: Misrepresents the notes by implying multiple exoplanets share each name, and it gives orbit facts instead of a generalization.
  • Choice B: Compares only the two planets' masses, so it does not make a broad claim about exoplanets.
  • Choice C: Generalizes about stars having designations and proper names, not about exoplanets.
  • Choice D: States that exoplanet names have diverse origins and supports the claim with Phobetor and Mastika.
The answer is D.
